title: Chiba DR-10 protocol droid

The '''Chiba DR-10 protocol droid''', sometimes known as the "'''Chiba Drio'''," was a small, spherical [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] produced by [[Chiba Corporation]], and preferred by [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomats]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Unlike most protocol [[Droid/Legends|droids]], the Chiba DR-10 floated on [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] and was equipped with a small, concealed [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. The droid was originally designed to be used for negotiations and [[Translator/Legends|translation]] for legal purposes, but it also saw service in shops and customs checkpoints.

However, the droid had a flaw where it became aggressive and irritable during negotiations it was losing, and sometimes the droid deployed its blaster without warning. The Empire exploited the flaw and used the droid for security and as a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], which was fought between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Jander Graffe]] used two Chiba DR-10 droids while on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]].

==Description==
The Chiba DR-10 [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] was a small, spherical [[Class three droid/Legends|class three droid]], which was equipped with [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] to hover over the ground. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was armed with a small, hidden [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], an unusual feature for a protocol droid. The weapon could be deployed quickly from a hidden compartment on the droid's underside<ref name="CCW" /> and had about the same range and power as a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]].<ref name="GAW" /> Due to the droid's small size and repulsorlifts, it was quite agile, but it was unable to lift much weight.<ref name="CCW" /> The droid had a small probe that it could use to manipulate objects.<ref name="GAW" />

In order to communicate over long distances, the DR-10 was equipped with an integrated [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. Some variants of the droid had a [[Recording rod|recording unit]] along with extensive [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] equipment, which included 360-degree, [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]], and telescopic vision as well as [[Motion sensor/Legends|motion sensors]].<ref name="CCW" /> Other models of the droid came equipped with [[translator unit]]s in order to [[Translator/Legends|interpret]] languages.<ref name="GAW" /> Some of the droids had [[Color/Legends|black-colored]] [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]], along with plating that was white, black [[Silver/Legends|silver]], and [[Copper/Legends|copper]]-colored. The droid had its photoreceptors on the sides of its spherical body, and it had a black-colored protrusion on its top side.<ref name="CCW" />

==Role==
Chiba DR-10 protocol droids were originally designed for the purposes of negotiation and [[Translator droid/Legends|translation]], tasks aided by the vast amount of information contained in the droids' [[Memory bank/Legends|memory banks]] about law, customs, trade, and business matters.<ref name="GAW" /> The droid had enough knowledge of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] law that it could provide individuals with legal advice.<ref name="CCW" /> Some of the droids had translator units to help in translation, and they were [[Programmer/Legends|programmed]] to [[Vocabulator/Legends|speak]] and understand [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], [[Binary/Legends|binary]], as well as five languages that could be chosen by the owner.<ref name="GAW" />

However, the Chiba DR-10 had a programming flaw that caused it to become more and more<ref name="GAW" /> sarcastic,<ref name="CCW" /> irritated, impatient, and aggressive when it had difficulties with obtaining the desired outcome in a negotiation.<ref name="GAW" /> The flaw was due to the fact that the droid had behavioral inhibitors that were programmed differently than those in other protocol droid models. Sometimes Chiba DR-10s became frustrated enough to deploy their blaster and start shooting, which frequently occurred without warning to others present.<ref name="GAW" /> The DR-10 also used the blaster when negotiations failed violently.<ref name="CCW" />

The droid's excellent perceptiveness also made it useful for security duty or as a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]].<ref name="CCW" /> Its small size, repulsorlifts, and its nature to ask questions before firing also made it useful for security tasks.<ref name="GAW" /> Despite the Chiba DR-10s' flaw, they were highly intelligent and could be very persuasive and diplomatic. However, the droids could also be quite deceptive.<ref name="GAW" /> Some units from the line were also programmed to use [[Computer/Legends|computers]] and to appraise the value of items. Some Chiba DR-10s were equipped with [[Heuristic processor/Legends|heuristic processors]],<ref name="CCW" /> which were expensive [[Processor/Legends|processors]] that allowed the droids to think creatively,<ref name="SECR 190-191">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]''</ref> but others only had [[basic processor]]s,<ref name="GAW" /> which did not allow for such versatility.<ref name="SECR 190-191" />

==History==
The Chiba DR-10 protocol droid, which was also known as the "Chiba Drio", was produced by [[Chiba Corporation]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the Galactic Empire. Units of the model could sell for prices of 9,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]<ref name="GAW" /> to 12,000 credits<ref name="CCW" /> for those who were allowed to own such restricted droids. The DR-10 was first used as a negotiator and translator droid for legal matters, but it was eventually used in not only law offices, but also in shops and custom checkpoints all throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="GAW" />

The droid was also used by the Galactic Empire in large numbers and was favored by Imperial functionaries and [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomats]] whose line of work took them into danger. Due to the droid's flawed programming, which was viewed as notorious by many, the forces of the Galactic Empire also used the droids as guards, probes, and for other security purposes.<ref name="GAW" /> [[Jander Graffe]], the [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sector]], had two DR-10s in his service when he was ordered by the Empire's ruler, [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], to fake [[Alien/Legends|alien]] riots on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]] in order to support raising security taxes there during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Party">{{WizCite|story=A Taxing Party|book=Coruscant and the Core Worlds}}</ref>

{{RPGseed}}
At one point, Moff Graffe's Chiba DR-10s were nearby when the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Deena Mipps]] and a group of individuals filmed a conference between Graffe and other government officials, a meeting where they plotted to cause riots among the alien population of Esseles to obtain more taxes for security purposes. Although they came close to stopping the Rebel team, the Chiba DR-10s and the Moff's other security forces were unable to do so.<ref name="Party" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Chiba DR-10 protocol droid was first featured in "[[A Taxing Party]]," an adventure seed that was contained in ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', a ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] in [[2003]]. The book was written by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[Chris Doyle]], [[Jason Fry]], [[Paul Sudlow]], [[John Terra]], and [[Daniel Wallace]] for the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook|revised edition]] of the ''Roleplaying Game''. In addition to being part of the adventure, the droid received a descriptive section in the book's Esseles entry with an illustration by [[Tommy Lee Edwards]].<ref name="CCW" /> In [[2009]], the Chiba DR-10 received an entry in ''[[Galaxy at War]]'', a book written by [[Rodney Thompson]], [[Gary Astleford]], [[Eric Cagle]], and Daniel Wallace for the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook|''Saga Edition'']] of Wizards' ''Star Wars Roleplaying Game''. For the ''Saga Edition'' of the ''Roleplaying Game'', the Chiba DR-10 was available for players to use for one of their characters.<ref name="GAW" />

While "A Taxing Party" mentioned Emperor Palpatine and the Rebel Alliance,<ref name="Party" /> neither ''Coruscant and the Core Worlds''<ref name="CCW" /> or ''Galaxy at War'' specified the era when the droids were in use.<ref name="GAW" /> Additionally, the ''Galaxy at War'' entry contained much information on the Chiba DR-10 that differed from its first entry. The cost changed<ref name="GAW" /> from 12,000 credits<ref name="CCW" /> to 8,000 credits, while the processor changed<ref name="GAW" /> from a heuristic processor<ref name="CCW" /> to a basic processor.<ref name="GAW" /> The droid's improved sensor equipment<ref name="CCW" /> was also removed and replaced with a translator unit.<ref name="GAW" /> This article treats the two different versions of the droid as two variants of the same type of droid.
